chapter_context.summary: Proclaims the restoration of Israel, the virgin rejoicing, Ephraim as a dear son, and announces the glorious promise of the New Covenant written on hearts.

outline.text: Everlasting Love Celebrating: Return of Israel with Mercy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Everlasting Love Celebrating: Return of Israel with Mercy (31:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Rachel Weeping for Her Children Comforted; Ephraim's Repentance
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rachel Weeping for Her Children Comforted; Ephraim's Repentance (31:10-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Sowing House of Israel; End of Inherited Guilt
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Sowing House of Israel; End of Inherited Guilt (31:23-30), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Great Promise of New Covenant Written on Hearts
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Great Promise of New Covenant Written on Hearts (31:31-34), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Unshakable Covenant of Nature and Rebuilt Holy City
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Unshakable Covenant of Nature and Rebuilt Holy City (31:35-40), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Conflict and Exile
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of conflict and exile, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 31, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
